 {"id":"franken_networkx-deh","title":"Drawing & Visualization: draw_networkx variants, LaTeX export, network_text","description":"## Scope
Implement ~15 drawing/visualization functions:
- `draw_networkx` — main drawing function (matplotlib)
- `draw_networkx_nodes` — draw nodes only
- `draw_networkx_edges` — draw edges only
- `draw_networkx_labels` — draw node labels
- `draw_networkx_edge_labels` — draw edge labels
- `draw_circular` — draw with circular layout
- `draw_kamada_kawai` — draw with Kamada-Kawai layout
- `draw_planar` — draw with planar layout
- `draw_random` — draw with random positions
- `draw_shell` — draw with shell layout
- `draw_spectral` — draw with spectral layout
- `draw_spring` — draw with spring layout
- `draw_forceatlas2` — draw with ForceAtlas2 layout
- `write_latex` / `to_latex` / `to_latex_raw` — export graph to LaTeX/TikZ
- `network_text` — ASCII art representation of graph

## Implementation Approach
- Most draw_* functions delegate to matplotlib with layout computation
- Can wrap NetworkX drawing directly for many of these
- network_text: ASCII tree/graph rendering, pure Python
- LaTeX export: generate TikZ code, pure Python string generation

## Implementation Location
- Python: `python/franken_networkx/drawing/`

## Test Requirements
- Verify draw functions don't error on valid graphs
- Verify LaTeX output is valid TikZ
- Verify network_text produces readable output
- Visual regression tests (optional)","status":"closed","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:14:33.237335091Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:38.812894650Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T03:36:38.812214806Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["drawing","tier-4","visualization"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"franken_networkx-deh","depends_on_id":"franken_networkx-l85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-21T19:16:02.736514279Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -13152,6 +13152,8 @@ def __init__(self, graph):
         self._graph = graph
         self.graph = graph.graph
         self.frozen = True
+        self.succ = _ReverseAdjacencyView(self)

     def __iter__(self):

@@ -13228,6 +13230,44 @@ def __getattr__(self, name):
         raise AttributeError(name)
 
 
+class _ReverseNeighborMap(Mapping):
+    def __init__(self, view, node, *, reverse=False):
+        self._view = view
+        self._node = node
+        self._reverse = reverse
+
+    def _raw_neighbors(self):
+        if self._reverse:
+            return self._view._graph.succ[self._node]
+        return self._view._graph.pred[self._node]
+
+    def __iter__(self):
+        return iter(self._raw_neighbors())
+
+    def __len__(self):
+        return len(self._raw_neighbors())
+
+    def __getitem__(self, neighbor):
+        return self._raw_neighbors()[neighbor]
+
+
+class _ReverseAdjacencyView(Mapping):
+    def __init__(self, view, *, reverse=False):
+        self._view = view
+        self._reverse = reverse
+
+    def __iter__(self):
+        return iter(self._view)
+
+    def __len__(self):
+        return len(self._view)
+
+    def __getitem__(self, node):
+        if node not in self._view._graph:
+            raise KeyError(f"Key {node} not found")
+        return _ReverseNeighborMap(self._view, node, reverse=self._reverse)
+
+
 _FILTERED_VIEW_MUTATORS = (

@@ -1,5 +1,6 @@
 """Tests for graph utility wrapper functions."""
 
+from collections.abc import Mapping
 import gc
 
 import networkx as nx
@@ -37,6 +38,12 @@ def _graph_snapshot(graph):
     )
 
 
+def _mapping_snapshot(value):
+    if isinstance(value, Mapping):
+        return tuple(sorted((repr(key), _mapping_snapshot(item)) for key, item in value.items()))
+    return value
+
+
 def _direction_utility_graph_pair(fnx_cls, nx_cls):
     graph = fnx_cls()
     expected = nx_cls()
@@ -2154,3 +2161,24 @@ def test_reverse_view_matches_networkx_without_fallback(monkeypatch, fnx_cls, nx
     assert result.is_directed() == expected_result.is_directed()
     assert result.is_multigraph() == expected_result.is_multigraph()
     assert _graph_snapshot(result) == expected_snapshot
+
+
+@pytest.mark.parametrize(
+    ("fnx_cls", "nx_cls"),
+    [
+        (fnx.DiGraph, nx.DiGraph),
+        (fnx.MultiDiGraph, nx.MultiDiGraph),
+    ],
+)
+def test_reverse_view_exposes_succ_and_pred_like_networkx_without_fallback(
+    monkeypatch, fnx_cls, nx_cls
+):
+    graph, expected = _view_utility_graph_pair(fnx_cls, nx_cls)
+    expected_result = nx.reverse_view(expected)
+
+    _block_networkx_utilities(monkeypatch, "reverse_view")
+
+    result = fnx.reverse_view(graph)
+
+    assert _mapping_snapshot(result.succ) == _mapping_snapshot(expected_result.succ)
+    assert _mapping_snapshot(result.pred) == _mapping_snapshot(expected_result.pred)
